{"query":"Rafah border crossing closed Israel latest date reason humanitarian impact Gaza 2025","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1298,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rafah_Border_Crossing","title":"Rafah Border Crossing - Wikipedia","content":"1906 Ottoman-British border agreement
On 26 November 2005, the Rafah crossing opened for the first time under the European Union's supervision.
On 25 June 2006, Palestinian militants attacked the Kerem Shalom border crossing and captured the Israeli soldier Gilad Shalit.
Consequently, since June 2007, the crossing has remained closed under the terms of the original agreement.
On 12 February 2007, PLO Negotiatior Saeb Erekat complained in a letter to the Israeli Government and the Head of the EU Mission about Israel, closing the Rafah Crossing Point (RCP) on most days by indirect measures, such as "preventing access by the EU BAM to the RCP through Kerem Shalom".
On 27 April, Fatah and Hamas reached an agreement in Cairo, mediated by Egypt and on 29 April, Egypt announced that the border crossing would be opened on a permanent basis.
Afterwards, it has been opened for a few days every few months.
October 2025 Gaza peace plan
In early May 2026, EUBAM Rafah reported that more than 4,000 passengers had crossed the Rafah Crossing Point since the mission's return on 2 February 2026, with roughly as many people leaving Gaza as entering Gaza.
After May 2011, when Egypt's President Hosni Mubarak was replaced with Mohamed Morsi, the number grew to 40,000 per month.
Between 24 October 2014 and September 2015, the crossing had been opened for only 34 days.
Archived from the original on 2025-02-12.
Here's why Cairo is reluctant to open it Archived 2023-10-15 at the Wayback Machine, CNN (October 15, 2023).

The Israeli government has described the closure as a ‘necessary security measure’ following Iranian missile strikes.
Read the April 2026 “Humanitarian Scorecard: Six Months In, Gaza Ceasefire is Failing” from Refugees International and partners here."},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/1/israel-closes-gazas-rafah-crossing-amid-attacks-on-iran","title":"Israel closes Gaza’s Rafah crossing amid attacks on Iran | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"Israel has closed Gaza’s Rafah border crossing with Egypt amid the joint Israeli-United States attacks on Iran.
“Several necessary security adjustments have been implemented, including the closure of the crossings into the Gaza Strip, among them the Rafah Crossing, until further notice,” Israel’s Coordinator of Government Activities in the Territories (COGAT) said in a statement.
It added that “the closure of the crossings will have no impact on the humanitarian situation in the Gaza Strip”, saying it would remain in contact with the international community and provide updates on any developments."},{"url":"https://www.nextcenturyfoundation.org/a-humanitarian-catch-22-reopening-of-the-rafah-crossing/","title":"A Humanitarian Catch 22: Reopening of the Rafah Crossing","content":"Jan 15, 2026 — Since 7 October 2023, more than 1,700 health workers have been killed and approximately 77% of Gaza's road network has been damaged, severely ...Read more"}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Rafah border crossing closed Israel latest date reason humanitarian impact Gaza 2025","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rafah_Border_Crossing","title":"Rafah Border Crossing","content":"The **Rafah Border Crossing** (Arabic: معبر رفح, romanized: *Ma`bar Rafaḥ*) or **Rafah Crossing Point** is the sole crossing point between Egypt and Palestine's Gaza Strip and Gaza's sole border point with a country other than Israel. The Rafah crossing was opened by Israel after the 1979 peace treaty and remained under Israeli control until 2005, when it was transferred to Egyptian, Palestinian Authority, and EU control, giving Palestinians partial control of an international border for the first time. Under a 2007 agreement between Egypt and Israel, Egypt controls the crossing but imports through the Rafah crossing require Israeli approval. Israel took control of the crossing on 7 May 2024, during the Gaza war, before withdrawing in January 2025 as part of a ceasefire agreement. On 2 February 2026, the crossing was reopened, but since the beginning of the Iran war, it was closed again. Before Israel's disengagement from Gaza on 11 September 2005, the main border crossing at Rafah between Egypt and Gaza was controlled by Israeli authorities.","score":0.8571119,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/3/1/israel-closes-gazas-rafah-crossing-amid-attacks-on-iran","title":"Israel closes Gaza’s Rafah crossing amid attacks on Iran | Israel-Palestine conflict News | Al Jazeera","content":"# Israel closes Gaza’s Rafah crossing amid attacks on Iran. *The crossing with Egypt is considered vital for the delivery of humanitarian aid and the evacuation of critically ill patients.*. JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel said Friday that it will reopen the pedestrian border crossing between the Gaza Strip and Egypt over the weekend, marking an important step forward for U.S. President Donald Trump's Gaza ceasefire plan.
